forecast-period.text: A ridge of high pressure will begin to extend from the west on Tuesday and will be briefly be weakened as a cold front crosses the southeast on Wednesday afternoon. The ridge will begin to re-establish in the wake of the front and stabilise over SA with the slow moving high pressure centre sitting south of WA by Friday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Isolated showers about the agricultural area and West Coast district, clearing in evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds.

Morning fog patches in the west. Isolated showers developing about the agricultural area and West Coast district, more frequent about the southern agricultural area afternoon and evening. Possible small hail in the evening about the far southeast. Maximum temperatures below average. Southwest to southeasterly winds turning northwesterly in the south ahead of a fresh and gusty southerly change late afternoon and evening, strong about southern coasts.

Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and West Coast district, scattered near southeastern and far western coasts. Possible small hail in the morning about the far southeast. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh southwest to southeasterly winds, gusty in the southeast.

text.p: Isolated showers about the agricultural areas and West Coast district. Maximum temperatures below average. Fresh southwest to southeasterly winds about the coast, easing to moderate inland.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: Dry in the far north with less than 2 mm over the southern pastorals, 1 to 5 mm over the northern agricultural areas, Eyre Peninsula & West Coast district. 2 to 10 mm about central districts, increasing to 5 to 15 mm about the southeast and Mount Lofty Ranges with localised falls up to 20mm about the far southeastern coastal fringes.
